description: FW-5: Move workspace_crates from global repo_metadata to per-branch storage. Introduces a new branch_metadata table (V14 migration), a BranchMetadataRepository, and rewires the orchestrator write site and graph read site to be keyed by branch_id — eliminating cross-branch contamination of internal-name resolution.

userStories.title: V14 migration creates branch_metadata table
userStories.description: As a storage maintainer, I need the new branch_metadata table and indices in place so subsequent stories can land their plumbing.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file crates/seshat-storage/migrations/V14__branch_metadata.sql with the schema from PRD Part II (B): columns branch_id TEXT NOT NULL, key TEXT NOT NULL, value TEXT NOT NULL, updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch()), PRIMARY KEY (branch_id, key), FOREIGN KEY (branch_id) REFERENCES branches(branch_id) ON DELETE CASCADE
Migration creates index idx_branch_metadata_branch_id ON branch_metadata(branch_id)
Migration uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS and CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS for idempotency
Migration applies cleanly on an empty DB
Migration applies cleanly on an existing DB already at V13
Migration test added to crates/seshat-storage/tests/migrations.rs (or equivalent fixture): applying V14 twice does not error
FK cascade verified by a test that inserts a branches row, inserts a branch_metadata row referencing it, deletes the branch, and asserts the metadata row is gone
After V14 applies, repo_metadata table is left unchanged (no destructive touch to existing rows)
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo test -p seshat-storage passes
userStories.notes: V14__branch_metadata.sql created. Tests added inline to crates/seshat-storage/src/db.rs::tests (the existing fixture — there is no tests/migrations.rs file in this crate; refinery embeds migrations and migration tests live alongside Database::open): extended migration_applies_on_fresh_in_memory_db to assert the new table + index, plus v14_migration_is_idempotent_on_reopen, v14_does_not_disturb_repo_metadata, v14_branch_metadata_cascades_on_branch_delete, v14_branch_metadata_primary_key_upserts_on_conflict. cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test -p seshat-storage passes (181/181); cargo clippy -p seshat-storage --tests -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: BranchMetadataRepository trait + Sqlite impl
userStories.description: As a scanner/graph crate consumer, I need a typed API to read/write per-branch metadata so callers do not sprinkle raw SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file crates/seshat-storage/src/repository/branch_metadata_repository.rs containing a BranchMetadataRepository trait

Trait methods: get(&self, branch_id: &str, key: &str) -> Result<Option<String>, _>; set(&self, branch_id: &str, key: &str, value: &str) -> Result<(), _>; list(&self, branch_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, _>; delete(&self, branch_id: &str, key: &str) -> Result<(), _>

Provide SqliteBranchMetadataRepository implementing the trait; set uses UPSERT semantics (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(branch_id, key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, updated_at = excluded.updated_at)
Module declared and re-exported from crates/seshat-storage/src/repository/mod.rs
Shape matches the existing repo_metadata_repository.rs (use it as the template)

Unit tests mirror repo_metadata_repository.rs::tests: get/set round-trip, overwrite via set, list returns all keys for a branch, delete removes the row, isolation between two different branch_ids (no cross-leak)
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
cargo test -p seshat-storage passes
userStories.notes: Trait lives in repository/mod.rs (next to RepoMetadataRepository, where every other trait already lives) rather than inside the impl file — keeps the convention consistent. Impl `SqliteBranchMetadataRepository` in repository/branch_metadata_repository.rs; UPSERT writes `updated_at = unixepoch()` on INSERT and copies it via `excluded.updated_at` on conflict. 8 unit tests added: set_and_get, get_missing_key, set_overwrites_existing, list_returns_all_keys_for_branch, list_empty_branch, delete_removes_row, delete_missing_key_is_noop, branches_are_isolated. Test fixture seeds `branches` rows up-front because the FK is enforced on INSERT (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON in Database::open). Re-exported via repository/mod.rs and lib.rs. cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test -p seshat-storage 189/189 (was 181, +8); cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean; cargo clippy -p seshat-storage --tests -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Orchestrator writes workspace_crates per-branch
userStories.description: As the scanner, when I finish a scan I must persist workspace_crates keyed by the current branch_id instead of the global repo_metadata slot.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace meta_repo.set("workspace_crates", &json) at crates/seshat-scanner/src/orchestrator.rs:501 with branch_meta.set(branch_id, "workspace_crates", &json) using the BranchMetadataRepository from US-002
branch_id is taken from OrchestratorContext (already in scope at that call site)
The old repo_metadata write for workspace_crates is removed (not duplicated)
Verify whether crates/seshat-watcher/src/hot_tier.rs shares this orchestrator entry point; if not, mirror the change there as well so incremental re-scans also write to branch_metadata
After a scan on branch_id = X, branch_metadata contains exactly one row (X, "workspace_crates", <json>)
Re-scanning on the same branch_id overwrites the value (UPSERT)
Re-scanning on a different branch_id adds a separate row — both rows coexist; neither overwrites the other
Update existing test scan_persists_workspace_crates_with_local_packages_union at crates/seshat-scanner/src/orchestrator.rs:1273 to assert the per-branch slot instead of the global repo_metadata slot
Add new test scan_two_branches_isolates_workspace_crates that scans against two different branch_ids with different fixture manifests and asserts no cross-contamination
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo test -p seshat-scanner passes
userStories.notes: Write site at crates/seshat-scanner/src/orchestrator.rs:500 now uses SqliteBranchMetadataRepository.set(&branch.0, "workspace_crates", &json) instead of the global SqliteRepoMetadataRepository.set. Imports in orchestrator.rs updated: RepoMetadataRepository / SqliteRepoMetadataRepository dropped from the lib's top-level use list and replaced with BranchMetadataRepository / SqliteBranchMetadataRepository (the test module still imports RepoMetadataRepository for the regression-guard assertion). hot_tier.rs verified — it does NOT call the workspace_crates write path (it only refreshes per-file IR), so no mirror change is needed there. Existing test scan_persists_workspace_crates_with_local_packages_union rewritten to read from branch_metadata and to assert repo_metadata['workspace_crates'] is now empty. New test scan_two_branches_isolates_workspace_crates added: two separate root dirs with different Cargo.toml manifests, scanned under main and feature branches; asserts cross-branch isolation, single-row UPSERT on re-scan, and that re-scanning one branch leaves the other's row untouched. cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test -p seshat-scanner passes (459 lib unit tests + integration tests, 2 new tests in lib); cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Graph reads workspace_crates per-branch
userStories.description: As the graph layer, when I resolve internal names I must read workspace_crates for the current branch_id instead of the global slot.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite load_internal_names at crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs:541 to read from branch_metadata via BranchMetadataRepository.get(branch_id, "workspace_crates") — branch_id is already a parameter, just stop ignoring it
Fall back to Vec::new() when the row is absent (preserve today's empty-list correctness for the no-manifest case)
Rename the existing test helper seed_workspace_crates_in_repo_metadata to seed_workspace_crates_in_branch_metadata and update its body to write to branch_metadata instead of repo_metadata
Update every call site of the renamed helper (use the rename to force visiting all tests in dependencies.rs::tests starting around line 1894) so they all seed the per-branch slot

Add new test query_dependencies_uses_per_branch_workspace_crates: seed two different branch_ids with different workspace_crates lists, run query_dependencies against each, assert divergent internal-name resolution
All existing tests in crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs::tests still pass
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo test -p seshat-graph passes
userStories.notes: load_internal_names at crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs:541 now reads from branch_metadata via SqliteBranchMetadataRepository.get(branch_id, "workspace_crates"), falling back to Vec::new() when the row is absent or the JSON does not parse. Top-of-file import switched from RepoMetadataRepository/SqliteRepoMetadataRepository to BranchMetadataRepository/SqliteBranchMetadataRepository — no test-module re-import needed because the test helper handles its own bindings. The existing test helper in dependencies.rs::tests was named seed_internal_names (not seed_workspace_crates_in_repo_metadata as the PRD wording suggested); renamed it to seed_workspace_crates_in_branch_metadata and rewired its body to (a) INSERT OR IGNORE the branches row first (V14 FK is enforced via PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON) and (b) write via SqliteBranchMetadataRepository.set. New required parameter `branch_id: &str` propagated to both call sites (query_dependencies_resolves_internal_crate_import_from_db and query_dependencies_external_import_not_resolved), both seeding 'main'. Added query_dependencies_uses_per_branch_workspace_crates: seeds 'main' with [crate_main] and 'feature' with [crate_feature], inserts a per-branch lib at crates/crate-main/src/foo.rs vs crates/crate-feature/src/foo.rs and a consumer at src/main.rs that imports the respective crate, then runs query_dependencies on each branch and asserts the cross-branch resolution is divergent (no cross-leak in either direction). cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test -p seshat-graph 297/297 (was 296, +1); cargo clippy -p seshat-graph --tests -- -D warnings clean; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: create_snapshot copies branch_metadata rows
userStories.description: As BranchRepository::create_snapshot, when a new branch is forked from an existing one its metadata must follow until the first scan refreshes it — otherwise queries on a freshly-snapshotted branch regress to empty until the watcher's warm tier triggers.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend BranchRepository::create_snapshot (crates/seshat-storage/src/repository/branch_repository.rs) to also copy branch_metadata rows: INSERT INTO branch_metadata (branch_id, key, value, updated_at) SELECT ?new_branch_id, key, value, updated_at FROM branch_metadata WHERE branch_id = ?source_branch_id
The new INSERT runs inside the same transaction as the existing nodes/edges/files_ir copies

Add new test create_snapshot_copies_branch_metadata in branch_repository.rs::tests, modelled on the existing create_snapshot_copies_nodes_and_files at line 311: seed branch_metadata for the source branch, snapshot, assert the rows appear under the new branch_id with identical key/value/updated_at
Existing snapshot tests continue to pass
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo test -p seshat-storage passes
userStories.notes: SqliteBranchRepository::create_snapshot at crates/seshat-storage/src/repository/branch_repository.rs now copies branch_metadata as part of the existing transaction, between the symbol_imports copy and tx.commit(): `INSERT INTO branch_metadata (branch_id, key, value, updated_at) SELECT ?1, key, value, updated_at FROM branch_metadata WHERE branch_id = ?2`. Doc comment captures the rationale (mirror the symbol-index reasoning: without this, internal-name resolution regresses to empty on a freshly snapshotted branch until the next full scan refreshes it). New test create_snapshot_copies_branch_metadata seeds two branch_metadata rows on main (uses ensure_branch_exists first so the V14 FK is satisfied), captures (key, value, updated_at) via raw SQL on the source side, snapshots to `feature-meta-snap`, then asserts the public list() API returns the expected key/value pairs AND the raw row tuples match the source row-for-row (including updated_at). Final assertion confirms the source's metadata is untouched by the copy. cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test -p seshat-storage 190/190 (was 189, +1); cargo clippy -p seshat-storage --tests -- -D warnings clean; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Cross-branch regression integration test
userStories.description: As a contributor, I want a locked-in regression test proving that switching branches no longer contaminates internal-name resolution.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new integration test crates/seshat-cli/tests/cross_branch_workspace_crates.rs (or extend the existing cross_branch_decisions.rs pattern from Epic 14 if that fits better)
Test sets up a fixture git repo with two branches main and feature
On main, Cargo.toml declares members = ["crate_a"]
On feature, Cargo.toml declares members = ["crate_a", "crate_b"]

Test scans each branch, switches between them, and asserts that query_dependencies against crate_a/src/lib.rs sees the right set of internal names on each branch (i.e. crate_b is internal on feature but external on main)
Test must run with embeddings disabled (--no-embeddings or equivalent mock) to keep runtime under 10 seconds
Existing cross_branch_decisions.rs integration test still passes (decisions are project-wide and this PRD does not touch them — this is a regression guard)
cargo check --workspace passes
cargo test -p seshat-cli passes
userStories.notes: New file crates/seshat-cli/tests/cross_branch_workspace_crates.rs added (1 test, workspace_crates_resolves_per_branch_across_real_git_branches). Real git repo via tempdir() + git init --initial-branch=main. Fixture: root Cargo.toml is a [workspace] manifest; on main members = ["crate_a"] (crate_b/ absent from disk), on feature members = ["crate_a","crate_b"] with crate_b/src/greet.rs (+ src/lib.rs). crate_a/src/lib.rs contains `use crate_b::greet::say;` — identical on both branches, so the difference in resolution comes purely from per-branch workspace_crates. Test scans each branch via scan_project (ScanConfig::default(), no embeddings — runtime 0.10s, well under the 10s budget), then runs seshat_graph::query_dependencies("crate_a/src/lib.rs") on each branch and asserts: (a) main's branch_metadata.workspace_crates contains crate_a but NOT crate_b; (b) feature's contains both; (c) scanning feature does not mutate main's row (cross-branch isolation guard); (d) on feature, query_dependencies returns a resolved dep pointing at crate_b/src/greet.rs; (e) on main, no dependency mentions crate_b AND all dependencies are unresolved (crate_a's only import is `crate_b::greet::say` which is external on main). Helpers (git, current_branch, init_repo, write_main_fixture, add_crate_b_on_feature) duplicated from cross_branch_decisions.rs / scan_records_head.rs per the existing per-file convention in tests/. cargo check --workspace passes; cargo test -p seshat-cli passes (all suites green; cross_branch_decisions still 8/8); cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Documentation, ADR, and CHANGELOG entry
userStories.description: As a future contributor, I want the schema decision recorded so nobody revisits (A) column-on-branches vs (B) new table.
acceptanceCriteria: Either append a Per-branch metadata section to the existing ADR at _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/14-1-merge-aware-decisions.md, OR create a new ADR _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/15-1-branch-metadata.md — document the (B) decision and why branch_metadata is preferred over an ALTER on the branches table
Add a [Unreleased] entry under ### Breaking in CHANGELOG.md describing the DB schema change (new V14 migration, repo_metadata.workspace_crates row no longer read)
Add a [Unreleased] entry under ### Added in CHANGELOG.md describing per-branch isolation of workspace_crates
Add a [Unreleased] entry under ### Fixed in CHANGELOG.md describing the cross-branch contamination bug that motivated the change
Update roadmap.md: mark the #fw5-branch-crates tag as IMPLEMENTED
Markdown lint / link checks on the touched docs pass (no broken cross-links)
